title: Dichapetalum sordidum
taxonomy: Dichapetalum sordidum is classified within the family Dichapetalaceae. This family is a small group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. Dichapetalum sordidum is a member of the genus Dichapetalum, which includes around 25 species of plants. The Dichapetalum genus is further classified within the order Oxalidales. The classification of Dichapetalum sordidum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

Its bark and leaves are believed to have anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. intro: Dichapetalum sordidum, also known as Poison Peach or Monkey Fruit, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its yellow or orange fruit and leaves with glandular hairs. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Dichapetalum sordidum include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dichapetalum sordidum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in the formulation of topical creams and ointments.
